page: Microsoft Azure DC4as_v6 is a virtual machine instance belonging to the DCasv6 server family. It is powered by an AMD processor utilizing the x86_64 architecture and runs on the Microsoft Hyper-V hypervisor. The instance features 4 dedicated virtual CPUs (vCPUs) paired with 16.0 GB of system memory, yielding a resource density of 4.0 GB of RAM per core.

This server configuration does not include any local storage, meaning all storage requirements must be met via external network-attached storage, though the instance is designated as Premium Storage capable. There are zero integrated GPUs, and the configuration does not include any complimentary public IPv4 addresses.

From a cost-efficiency perspective, the dedicated CPU allocation and balanced 1:4 vCPU-to-RAM ratio position this instance as a stable general-purpose resource. It is designed for workloads that require consistent CPU performance without the variability of burstable instances, such as web serving, microservices, and small database hosting, provided that external storage is attached.
description: Microsoft Azure DC4as_v6 is an x86_64 virtual machine from the DCasv6 family, running on the Microsoft Hyper-V hypervisor. It features 4 dedicated AMD vCPUs and 16.0 GB of system memory, establishing a balanced 1:4 CPU-to-RAM ratio. The instance lacks local storage, requiring external Premium Storage volumes for data persistence, and contains no integrated GPUs or complimentary public IPv4 addresses. With its dedicated CPU allocation, the server provides stable, predictable compute resources. It is configured for general-purpose workloads, including web serving, application hosting, and microservices, where consistent CPU performance is required and external storage configurations are acceptable.
og_description: Microsoft Azure DC4as_v6 is an x86_64 virtual machine featuring 4 dedicated AMD vCPUs, 16 GB RAM, and Premium Storage capability, designed for general-purpose workloads without local storage.
